rulings.Case Title: RONALD PANKOWSKI VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, March 16, 2018, Line 1. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Granted. Plaintiff Pankowski's single cause of action for intentional interference fails because the court does not find that application of the doctrine of promissory estoppel to the city's actions to avoid injustice to plaintiff outweighs the severe adverse impacts on public policies promoted by the City's procedural requirements for contracting to be in writing and in a form approved by the city attorney. Plaintiff was acting on the oral and written representation of a public official, no established procedure was followed, and the city's failure to honor the promise made in the representation does not deviate from any approved standards or procedures, and the plaintiff has not suffered the substantial private hardship. See Schafer v. City of Los Angeles (2015) 237 Cal.App.4th 1250, 1263; State of California v. Haslett Co. (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 252, 257; HPT IHG-2 Properties Trust v. City of Anaheim (2015) 243 Cal.App.4th 188. =(501/REQ)

Demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/REQ)
